frameworks.name: Gaussian population receptive field model
frameworks.one_line: Voxel response modeled as Gaussian over feature space, parametrized by mean and size.

frameworks.name: optimization theory
frameworks.one_line: Goodness-of-fit landscape analyzed for sensitivity to pRF mean vs size parameters.

frameworks.name: bias-variance decomposition
frameworks.one_line: pRF size estimation framed as a bias-variance tradeoff problem.

core_question: Why is the Gaussian pRF size estimate unreliable within and between subjects, and can the reliability be improved without sacrificing pRF mean estimation?
key_assumptions: Voxel response is well-approximated by a Gaussian shape in feature space
pRF reliability can be quantified separately for mean and size parameters
Simulated and real fMRI data share the same estimator behavior
manipulations: Estimation procedure (single best-fit Gaussian vs linear combination of similar-fit models vs permutation approach)
key_findings: pRF mean estimates are highly reliable but pRF size estimates are inconsistent within and between subjects
Goodness-of-fit is less sensitive to variation in pRF size than to variation in pRF mean
Linear combination of similar-fit pRF models and a permutation-based approach improve pRF size reliability
The reliability improvement for size does not degrade pRF mean reliability or prediction accuracy
interpretations: pRF size inconsistency is an inherent property of the Gaussian pRF objective rather than a noise artifact
Aggregating across similar-fit models or permutations is a viable remedy for the size estimation problem
